Foundation campaign partnership proposal

Tonga Heritage Squad Campaign

A four month campaign supporting junior rugby league players to be Loto Lahi about being Tongan, building towards the two day Heritage Cup Tournament on September 26 and 27, 2026.

78+ kidsU7 to U12 with older age groups added next year.
4 monthsTraining, media, culture and tournament preparation.
R4W kitTraining kit becomes the campaign identity.
CultureFamily, faith, Tonga pride and player development.

The campaign opportunity

This is not a one-off logo placement. It is a visible community campaign where R4W can be seen across training, content, cultural moments, trial game promotion, jersey presentation and the tournament build-up.

The Tonga squad is a community pathway that will look for more junior rugby playing opportunities beyond one tournament, with this foundation year building the platform for older age groups next year.

Scout visibility

Heritage events, trial games and cultural rugby league fixtures are the type of moments scouts love to attend because they can see young NSW talent under pressure, with pride, identity, family support and competitive energy all in one place.

Campaign journey

1

Training period months 1 to 4

Weekly preparation in R4W branded training kit. Content begins once kits are received and paid film is booked.

2

R4W owned trial game

R4W becomes the Presenting Partner with full kit ownership for the match, including the Sipi Tau moment and trial game content used to promote next year’s growth.

3

Jersey presentation night

Players receive their game day kit, families gather, sponsor recognition is captured and player stories become content.

4

Two day tournament

September 26 and 27. Six age groups. One Tonga campaign. Community pride brought to the field.

5

Post campaign impact

R4W receives a reach report, content assets and public founding partner recognition.

Community event moment

Jersey presentation event

This event gives R4W a polished cultural and community moment before the tournament. Players, families, sponsors and supporters gather in one room while the squad receives their game day kit, presents in Tongan attire and creates content that can be shared across the campaign.

Opening prayerSetting the foundation with faith, unity and purpose.
Player introductionsPlayers share their name, village and age group connection.
Media momentsPlayer profiles, family interviews, highlight footage and sponsor recognition.
Cultural attire ceremonyPlayers and coaching staff presented together in full Tongan attire.
Traditional performanceCulture, movement, pride and identity captured for social content.
Community recognitionAcknowledging supporters who back the players, families and campaign.

Why it matters for R4W: this is the sponsor content engine. It creates the emotional story behind the kit before the trial game, Sipi Tau moment and September tournament.

Powered by influence. Shared by community.

The influencer layer gives the campaign movement without making the presentation look like screenshots. These creators help the R4W kit and Tonga story reach audiences already connected to Pacific humour, sport, family, rugby league and culture.

What R4W receives

  • R4W branded training kit exposure across the full four month campaign
  • Presenting Partner position for the R4W owned trial game
  • Sipi Tau, training, player profile, jersey night and tournament content
  • TikTok influencer sharing and endorsement of the training kit campaign
  • Brand review before public content featuring R4W is posted
  • Full campaign reach report and content package after the tournament

Investment options

Founding Partner $10,000
Age Group Partner All 6 Groups $12,000
Full Program Ownership $22,000

The strongest position is full program ownership because it gives R4W the cleanest story: the brand that backed Tonga from the foundation campaign.

Full program ownership

Brand control with real community impact.

As Full Program Owner, R4W Active is not simply sponsoring the campaign. You are helping shape it.

The most important cost is securing all 78 junior player registrations so every child reaches the Heritage Cup. By covering that cornerstone cost, R4W becomes the partner that helped get the kids on the field.

Fa’iteliha means freedom to lead. If R4W wants stronger brand placement, campaign direction, or front-of-jersey presence on game day jerseys, the subcommittee is open to supporting that vision.

What R4W can own
Front-of-jersey optionBrand placement on official game day jerseys if R4W wants that position.
78 registrations supportedThe key cost that ensures every junior player reaches the tournament.
Trial match presentationR4W owns the trial game moment, including kit visibility and the Sipi Tau.
Campaign directionThe subcommittee supports R4W’s direction on brand presentation and activation.
Founding partner legacyRecognition as the partner who backed Tonga from the beginning.
One campaign. One nation. One future.
Back the kids.
Back Tonga.
Be Loto Lahi.

R4W Active has the opportunity to own the foundation moment before the campaign grows into older age groups next year.
